content_preview.title: FDA Grants Fast-Track Designation to First Bioengineered Liver for Acute Liver Failure After Phase 1 Trial Shows Promise
content_preview.summary: United Therapeutics' miroliverELAP, an external bioengineered liver built on a decellularized pig scaffold seeded with human cells, receives FDA RMAT designation after all five Phase 1 patients survived treatment.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has granted Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy (RMAT) designation to miroliverELAP, an investigational external liver assist product developed by Miromatrix Medical Inc., a wholly owned subsidiary of United Therapeutics Corporation. The designation, [announced on April 8, 2026](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260408473035/en/United-Therapeutics-Receives-FDA-Regenerative-Medicine-Advanced-Therapy-Designation-for-miroliverELAP-for-Tre...

recommendations: 1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent regenerative medicine article covering RMAT designation and Phase 1 trial results. Clear explanation of decellularized scaffold technology.
editor_notes.source_verification: 3 BusinessWire sources. Source 0 (RMAT announcement): HTTP 403 — primary source for the April 8 designation. Source 1 (Phase 1 results): VERIFIED via Archive.org snapshot — confirms 5 ALF patients, 44 hours continuous treatment, safety endpoint met, 32-day follow-up, Jeff Ross quote verbatim, no adverse events, mirokidney development. Source 2 (first patient): captured via Archive.org — confirms June 2025 first patient at Intermountain Medical Center. Human-requested article reviewed with heightened scrutiny.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Phase 1 trial details verified against BusinessWire press releases: 5 patients, 44 hours, safety endpoint, Jeff Ross quote. RMAT designation details consistent with the 21st Century Cures Act framework. Source 0 (RMAT announcement) returned 403 but is a primary company press release — legitimate source.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and appropriately cautious about Phase 1 limitations. No overclaiming of efficacy.
editor_notes.originality: Distinct from prior organ bioengineering coverage. Cross-references xenotransplantation article appropriately.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong human-requested biomedical article. Core claims verified via Archive.org snapshots. Approved.
